approvals.statute: Premerger notification required under 15 U.S.C. § 18a if size-of-transaction (>$119.5M indexed) and size-of-person thresholds are met. Initiates a 30-day waiting period (15 days for cash tender offers).
approvals.notes: The transaction requires 'HSR Act waiting period expiration or termination' as a remaining closing condition.

status_history.excerpt: The transaction requires 'HSR Act waiting period expiration or termination' as a remaining closing condition.
Both parties filed HSR Act notifications on February 13, 2026, with the waiting period scheduled to expire March 16, 2026.
HSR 30-day waiting period expired on 2026-03-15 with no second request or litigation detected.

approvals.statute: FTC substantive antitrust review. FTC typically reviews pharma, healthcare, consumer goods, and retail sectors. May issue consent order, accept divestitures, or issue administrative complaint and seek preliminary injunction.
approvals.notes: The merger agreement requires 'HSR Act waiting period expiration or termination' and 'antitrust consents/approvals in certain other jurisdictions' as remaining conditions.

status_history.excerpt: The merger agreement requires 'HSR Act waiting period expiration or termination' and 'antitrust consents/approvals in certain other jurisdictions' as remaining conditions.

approvals.statute: Stockholder approval required in most public company mergers. Threshold depends on merger structure and state of incorporation (typically majority of shares outstanding for Delaware long-form mergers).
approvals.conditions: Definitive proxy and meeting dates contingent on SEC Form S-4 registration statement becoming effective
approvals.notes: PEN stockholder approval (majority of outstanding voting power) is listed as a remaining closing condition, with a Form S-4 registration statement required for the stock consideration component.

status_history.excerpt: PEN stockholder approval (majority of outstanding voting power) is listed as a remaining closing condition, with a Form S-4 registration statement required for the stock consideration component.

approvals.statute: Form S-4 registration statement required for stock-for-stock mergers; proxy statement (Schedule 14A) required for cash mergers requiring target stockholder vote. SEC staff review typically takes 30 days; comments must be resolved before effectiveness.
approvals.conditions: SEC must declare registration statement effective before definitive proxy can be mailed and meeting dates set
approvals.notes: 'Effectiveness of BSX Form S-4 registration statement with the SEC' is listed as a remaining closing condition.

status_history.excerpt: 'Effectiveness of BSX Form S-4 registration statement with the SEC' is listed as a remaining closing condition.

The specific calendar dates will be populated in the definitive proxy statement once the SEC registration statement becomes effective, referencing a preliminary proxy statement dated February 27, 2026.

approvals.statute: FDA marketing authorizations (NDA, BLA, 510(k)) transfer with the legal entity but manufacturing site changes require FDA notification. CMS Change of Ownership (CHOW) required for each Medicare/Medicaid provider number. May require state-level CHOW in parallel.

EC merger review under EU Merger Regulation (EUMR) 139/2004. One-stop-shop for EU-wide transactions. Phase I: 25 working days. Phase II: additional 90 working days. Can be extended for remedies. Member state referrals possible under Article 9 (to national CA) or Article 22 (from national CAs to EC).
approvals.conditions: Pending referral noted — possible member state referral to or from European Commission under EUMR Article 9 or 22
approvals.notes: The transaction requires 'antitrust consents/approvals in certain other jurisdictions' beyond the HSR, implying EU and potentially other international filings are anticipated.

status_history.excerpt: The transaction requires 'antitrust consents/approvals in certain other jurisdictions' beyond the HSR, implying EU and potentially other international filings are anticipated.

tracker_fields.notes: 50 calendar days from January 14, 2026 = approximately March 5, 2026

approvals.statute: State attorneys general can independently investigate and challenge mergers under state antitrust law. Can seek injunctions in state or federal court. Most active AGs: California (DOJ), New York (AG), Texas (AG), Illinois (AG), Colorado (AG), Massachusetts (AG).

deal_events.description: BSX must file and obtain SEC effectiveness of Form S-4 before stockholder vote can occur; no filing date disclosed yet
deal_events.excerpt: Effectiveness of BSX Form S-4 registration statement with the SEC

deal_events.excerpt: PEN stockholder approval (majority of outstanding voting power)

deal_events.excerpt: HSR Act waiting period expiration or termination; antitrust consents/approvals in certain other jurisdictions

deal_events.description: NYSE listing approval for BSX shares issued as stock consideration is a parallel closing condition
deal_events.excerpt: NYSE listing approval for BSX shares to be issued as stock consideration

deal_events.description: Penumbra entered into a definitive Agreement and Plan of Merger with Boston Scientific Corporation and wholly owned subsidiary Pinehurst Merger Sub, Inc.
deal_events.excerpt: Penumbra, Inc. (PEN) entered into an Agreement and Plan of Merger with Boston Scientific Corporation (BSX) and its wholly owned subsidiary Pinehurst Merger Sub, Inc.

deal_events.description: Mixed cash/stock deal: $374.00/share cash for 73.26% of shares; 3.8721 BSX shares per PEN share for 26.74% of shares, subject to proration; no financing condition
deal_events.excerpt: Cash consideration: $374.00 per share (for 73.26% of outstanding shares); Stock consideration: 3.8721 BSX shares per PEN share (for 26.74% of outstanding shares)

deal_events.description: Company termination fee set at $525M; Parent reverse termination fee for antitrust-related failure set at $900M
deal_events.excerpt: Parent owes a $900M reverse termination fee for antitrust-related failure, while the Company owes a $525M termination fee under specified circumstances.

deal_events.description: PEN Board retains right to change recommendation or terminate for a superior proposal subject to $525M termination fee
deal_events.excerpt: Fiduciary out provision permits the PEN Board to change recommendation or terminate for a superior proposal (subject to $525M termination fee)

deal_events.description: Preliminary unaudited Q4/FY2025 financial results disclosed in conjunction with merger announcement
deal_events.excerpt: Preliminary Q4/FY2025 financial results disclosed but unaudited; actual results may differ

deal_events.description: Expected close updated: unknown → 2026 (management target), with Initial Outside Date of January 14, 2027
Both parties filed HSR Act notifications on February 13, 2026; initial 30-day waiting period scheduled to expire March 16, 2026
deal_events.excerpt: Both parties filed HSR Act notifications on February 13, 2026, with the waiting period scheduled to expire March 16, 2026.

deal_events.description: Preliminary proxy statement filed February 27, 2026; SEC effectiveness pending before definitive proxy can be mailed and meeting dates set
deal_events.excerpt: The specific calendar dates will be populated in the definitive proxy statement once the SEC registration statement becomes effective.

deal_events.description: Special Meeting date and Record Date formally set by Board but remain blank placeholders in preliminary proxy; meeting to be held in-person at Alameda, CA
deal_events.excerpt: The Record Date is established as [blank], 2026... The Special Meeting is scheduled for [blank], 2026 at 10:00 a.m. Pacific Time at Building 1310, One Penumbra Place, Alameda, CA 94502.

deal_events.description: Non-U.S. filings required to be initiated within 50 calendar days of January 14, 2026 Merger Agreement (deadline approximately March 5, 2026)
deal_events.excerpt: Non-U.S. filings must be initiated within 50 calendar days of the January 14, 2026 Merger Agreement date.

deal_events.description: Initial Outside Date is January 14, 2027 (12 months from signing); Boston Scientific committed to $900M reverse termination fee if regulatory approvals not obtained within 24 months (by January 14, 2028)
deal_events.excerpt: The merger agreement establishes an Initial Outside Date of January 14, 2027 (12 months from signing), with extension provisions available to either party.

deal_events.description: Both Boston Scientific and Penumbra filed HSR Act premerger notifications on February 13, 2026; 30-day waiting period set to expire March 16, 2026
deal_events.excerpt: Both parties filed HSR Act notifications on February 13, 2026, with the waiting period scheduled to expire March 16, 2026.

deal_events.description: Penumbra filed preliminary proxy statement (Form S-4 or Schedule 14A) with SEC dated February 27, 2026; SEC review and effectiveness pending
deal_events.excerpt: The specific calendar dates will be populated in the definitive proxy statement once the SEC registration statement becomes effective.

deal_events.description: 10-day VWAP ending two trading days prior to closing confirmed as BSX stock price measurement period; fixed proration of 73.26% cash / 26.74% stock confirmed; no fractional shares, cash in lieu based on same VWAP
deal_events.excerpt: The Boston Scientific stock price for consideration purposes is determined using a 10-day volume-weighted average price (VWAP) ending two trading days prior to closing, sourced from Bloomberg L.P.

deal_events.description: EU competition law clearance required and described as subject to pending referral; non-U.S. filings required within 50 days of signing (approximately March 5, 2026 deadline)
deal_events.excerpt: Non-U.S. approvals include EU competition law clearance (subject to pending referral) and competition/foreign investment approvals in certain other jurisdictions.

deal_events.description: Management publicly targets 2026 close; Initial Outside Date January 14, 2027; reverse termination fee backstop extends to January 14, 2028
deal_events.excerpt: Management expects the transaction to close in 2026, as stated in the proxy statement filed following the January 14, 2026 merger agreement signing.

deal_events.event_type: No Competing Bids / No Union Issues
deal_events.description: No competing bids or inbound interest reported; no union or collective bargaining matters disclosed in proxy excerpts
deal_events.excerpt: No union-related matters are specifically disclosed in the provided SEC filing excerpts... the excerpts contain no references to collective bargaining agreements, union contracts, labor organizations, work stoppages, or union consultation requirements.

management_commentary.commentary: The absence of a financing condition removes funding risk; the large $900M reverse break fee provides protection to PEN shareholders against antitrust-related deal failure. Timeline depends on SEC registration statement effectiveness, stockholder vote, and antitrust clearances across multiple jurisdictions.

Management expects the transaction to close in 2026 with an Initial Outside Date of January 14, 2027 and a $900M reverse termination fee backstop extending to January 14, 2028 for regulatory failure; the proxy cautions that actual timing depends on stockholder approval, HSR clearance, other regulatory approvals, and SEC registration statement effectiveness.

expected_close: 2026 (management target), with Initial Outside Date of January 14, 2027
